name: Kentucky Burgoo
recipeIngredient: 3 lb Ready to Cook Broiler Chicken
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 10-quart Dutch oven or stock pot, combine chicken, beef cross-cuts, water, salt, and pepper. Cover and cook until the meat is tender, about 1 hour.
2. Remove chicken and beef from the broth, reserving the broth. Remove the chicken and beef from the bones; discard the skin and bones. Cube the beef and chicken. Set aside.
3. Cook bacon in a skillet until crisp; drain on paper towels, reserving drippings. Crumble the bacon and set aside.
4. To the reserved broth in the Dutch oven, add cubed beef, undrained tomatoes, potatoes, carrots, onion, celery, green pepper, sugar, red pepper, cloves, garlic, and Bay Leaf. Cover and simmer for 1 hour, stirring often.
5. Remove the cloves and bay leaf. Using a knife, make cuts down the center of each row of corn kernels and scrape off the cobs. Add corn, cubed chicken, undrained beans, and okra to the Dutch oven; simmer for 20 minutes.
6. Blend flour with the reserved bacon drippings; stir into the stew. Cook until the stew thickens. Salt to taste. Garnish with parsley and serve hot with baking powder biscuits for a great meal.
